TextENGNew: ...No matter how many times I thought back on it, my anger never subsided.
``...God damn it!!``
After entering the break room, I made sure nobody else was inside, then slammed the personnel change letter in my hand down on the floor.
It made no sound. Just an empty response as it slid and danced along the floor, before stopping at the foot of the sofa a short distance away.
``What is this...? What the hell are they saying?!``
I could not understand the meaning of this.
The transfer notice itself was messy and inconsistent, the timing was far too abrupt, and the circumstances leading up to it were totally random.
As a government official, I realized that personnel changes were something of a regular occurrence... but this particular appointment was completely unacceptable.
``Why am I being sent to Public Relations...?!``
This may be conceited of me to say... but ever since I was assigned to the career group, I was especially proud of my work in Public Security.
Fighting against terrorist groups masquerading as citizens organizations, and false political groups that exist only to line their own pockets.
And even foreign intelligence agencies meddling with domestic politics from behind the scenes...
All of them were tough assignments, and there was always risk of death involved in these investigations.
There was hardly any time off, and it even caused me to break up with the girlfriend I was dating before I was appointed there.
But even so... I felt a sense of purpose toward the mission, that my duties were worthwhile.
That is why I accepted the total lack of free time and being buried in work until the moment I headed to sleep, and yet...!

``Why Public Relations of all places?!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2125. There's no danger, no creativity in it. It's just feeding information to the press?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2126. Hah, what a do-nothing-job!!``
So, I cried out.
I could not help shout...
But just then...
``...There you are. I was looking for you.``
As he uttered those words... my boss, Commissioner Yamaoki, entered the room.
Then he noticed the personnel change letter on the floor next to the sofa, picked it up and dusted it off, then handed it to me.

``It's common practice to hand your personnel change letter over to your new boss.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2003. Defacing it or getting it dirty would call your abilities into question.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2004. ...Shouldn't a member of the career group like yourself already know that?``
I suppressed my exploding anger as I snatched it from him.
...Even so, I failed to properly control my strength and got a little rough as I did.
Yamaoki-san turned around and bought two cans of coffee from the nearby vending machine.
Then he said ``here`` as he tossed one my way.
``Ah... it's hot?``
I caught the can on instinct... but was surprised as soon as I felt the heat and nearly dropped it.
Yamaoki-san watched me do so out of the corner of his eyes as he took a seat of the sofa beside me, pulled the tab, took a sip, and then turned back toward me.
``...This must be hard on you.``
``Obviously...!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2130. What is Personnel Affairs thinking?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2131. Did I do something wrong?!``
``Come on, calm down.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2007. ...Just have a drink and cool off for now.``
``I can't drink something hot in this goddamn heat!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2133. I'm fine!``
I slammed the can of coffee down on the table in front of Yamaoki-san as I uttered those words.
...He was not surprised at all, though. He just rolled the can around in his hand and shrugged his shoulders.
``Hmm... so you hate Public Relations then?``
``It's not a matter of likes and dislikes!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2135. I have a little confidence in my work for Public Security, and I take pride in it!``
``Plus, I'd rather take the lead and handle things on my own instead of feeling sorry for the subordinates who are put at risk in the line of duty!``
``And yet, moving on to a desk job at PR is relegating me to the posterior... I can't accept this!``
``Hoh, ``PR`` and ``posterior,`` huh?@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2010. ...That's some cute wordplay.``
``I'm being serious here!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2139. If you came here to make fun of me, I'm out!!``
For the time being, I faintly bowed my head and then turned around to find somewhere else to vent my anger.
I should head out early today and drown my sorrows in alcohol.
...I reached for the doorknob as I muttered that to myself, intending to leave the room.
But then.
``...You'd have your current position erased,@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2012. without even understanding the situation?``
When he called out to me in that low voice, I suddenly turned back and caught my breath.
Yamaoki-san sipped at his coffee while silently pointing at the sofa in front of him, with a look in his eyes clearly telling me to have a seat.
I could not defy an order from my boss, so I took a seat as directed.
...Of course, I still did not feel like drinking the coffee in front of me.
``What do you mean... by erased?``

``Exactly as it sounds.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2014. We recently noticed some strange activity at CIRO... the Cabinet Information Research Office.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2015. Other sources tell me that they're speaking to information sources from your investigation, performing a double inquiry.``

``Asking the same questions to the same people... Even though they could have just asked us for the information, they're intentionally doing things in a roundabout and inefficient way.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2017. ...And what's more, our work at Public Security is confidential.``
``...So, in other words.``
``That's right.``
Yamaoki-san gave me an immediate confirmation.

CIRO is an institution acting under orders from the Prime Minister and other cabinet ministers, that handles information gathering and investigation, plus operation and scheming for top secret activities related to politics, business, and the government.
In other words, the culprits or key witnesses I was pursuing... were government officials.
And what's more, these were people in fairly high positions.
``H-hold on...!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2145. It's true that Public Security has gotten involved in some dangerous situations, but we were all acting under orders from the big shots, right?``
``And yet, why would they be focusing on someone like me?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2147. Wouldn't it be more effective to put that pressure on the big shots instead?``

``Right... it certainly seems there's no problem with the case you were in charge of.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2020. ...However, you should have an idea what the issue is.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2021. You went on vacation a few weeks ago... Where was it exactly that you went?``
``Th-that's...!``
I got flustered and attempted to make excuses, but... after a quick glance at Yamaoki's sharp gaze, I immediately realized it was useless.
He of all people probably already knew what I was doing on my time off.
``...Yamaoki-san, I didn't realize you made a hobby of snooping.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2150. If I were a woman, I'd be filing a complaint about violating my privacy.``
``...@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2023.You should've been ordered to cease investigation into Deputy Inspector Minai's case.``
``I've kept this to myself for now, but it would be problematic if the higher-ups learned about it.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2025. Not to mention, if it's driven even CIRO to act, then...``
``You're saying I might end up like Minai-san...? Is that it?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2152. ...I see, so you want me to flee somewhere far away until things cool down?``
``Thank you for your concern, but I'm just a humble detective.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2154. I'm well aware of the danger...``
``Use your head, you idiot!@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2027. Do you want to avenge Minai or not?@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2028. Or is this all just an act? Well?!``
Being struck with that sharp, earnest attack, I could not help freeze on the spot.
...The hot air that had me sweating just a moment ago receded immediately, and the anger and feeling that I was standing on a slope quickly disappeared.
``...Maeno.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2030. You're looking down on Public Relations.``
``You may think it makes you a glorified clerk handing documents over to the press, but you're making a huge mistake there.``
``You weren't put there to sit idly by.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2033. Not even to keep you out of danger.``
``You've been given an important mission, to build up our strength in the police force to overcome authority.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2035. Try using your head a little more.``
``The strength... to overcome authority?``
``Yes.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2037. Do you remember the Shirakaba mountain villa?``
``Yes, of course.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2158. How could I ever forget?``
...That day, I was shot in the knee by one of the perpetrators, Kazunori Takiguchi.
Even to this day, I am unable to sprint thanks to the wound I suffered back then.
But... truth be told, I should've died from Takiguchi's second shot.
I was saved by my subordinate... who I considered a blood brother, the now departed Deputy Inspector Minai.
``That case was different from the T University siege incident... we had public opinion on our side.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2039. Do you understand why that is?``
``Of course... The perpetrators were an armed criminal group that had taken hostages. And they had even shot two police officers and one civilian, right?``
``There's no way to gain the support of the masses after committing such a heinous crime.``
``Indeed, that's one way of looking at it.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2041. ...However, there's one more thing to remember.``

``Though there were no deaths in the T University siege incident, there were many serious and minor injuries among both the students and the police.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2043. Nevertheless, public opinion remained in support of the students.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2044. ...So, where does the difference lie?``
As soon as Yamaoki-san pointed that out, I could guess what he was getting at.
Unlike the T University siege incident last year, MPD had intentionally set up a public relations officer to handle the press during the Shirakaba mountain villa incident.

The commanding officer in charge of the raid was killed in the line of duty by sniper fire, so Yamaoki-san was temporarily placed in command...@k but his original role was to handle media correspondence.
``At the time, I worked with the general manager of Public Relations and the NPS chief of staff Yuuki, striving to organize the information being sent to the press.``

``And we asked the media companies to put together programming focusing on the families of the two officers that died in the line of duty, rather than focusing on the criminal who was shot and killed by a police officer.``
...As a result, the police certainly did still receive criticism for taking inappropriate countermeasures against armed criminals.
But at the same time, public outrage against the armed criminal group increased. And most viewers asked about the police shooting during the incident felt that it was a justified act of self-defense.
In that situation, the opposition was unable to speak out about the police officer opening fire, so the topic was shelved as a future issue...
``...@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2163.That's how Minai-san got off with just a warning, wasn't it?``

``Right.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2048. And, there are other points.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2049. In the beginning, some of them were acting independently of their leader, and planning to escape by taking the landlord's wife hostage.``
``...But they gave up when they found out their comrades had already been arrested.``
So... what would have happened in the siege incident if the criminal group had never heard what happened to their comrades?
They'd probably steal a car or something and try to escape, banking on us prioritizing the life of the hostages... probably hoping to hide somewhere and bide their time until they could start over.
At that point, they may have killed the hostages they'd taken to silence them... In a worst-case scenario like that, the reputation of the police would've plummeted.
...But without their leadership, they became anxious about the remainder of their plan, so they stopped thinking.
There may have been some conflict of opinions with the next-in-command.
So they continued stepping back. They did not make any demands of the siege officers, and they could not even think of securing another escape route...
``And then, when they heard the victims' immediate family members talking on TV and saw the state of the outside world, they became increasingly aware that their actions were no longer justifiable.``

``Thanks to that, they were willing to confess during subsequent interrogation.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2053. ...It's no exaggeration to say that the power of the press played a major role in resolving the incident.``
``...Indeed, that's certainly true.``
That was not taken into account during the T University incident... the press reporters and photographers were just secured in a designated safe zone.
And thanks to that, their cameras only captured video of students conducting heroic demonstration activities, so the reporters even described it as ``the dawn of a new era`` in their articles.
On the other hand, the actions of the police, especially the riot police were regarded as ``evil`` displays of power, so the general public criticized their oppressive demeanor.
...Yamaoki of all people recognized the power and the value of the mass media, more than anyone.

``But it's far too new a field... so you can't expect the upper ranks to understand it.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2055. I, myself, don't have enough knowledge or sufficient contacts when it comes to public relations.``

``That's why... we need to build a steady, solid foundation in that field.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2057. ...It's a big responsibility, and demands a strong ability to act.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2058. So, do you still consider this post a demotion...?``
``In other words... Yamaoki-san,@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2166. you had me assigned here... to spearhead that effort?``
``Yes.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2060. It's not just about avenging Minai-kun... You're here because the mass media will inevitably be both beneficial and detrimental in future investigations.``

``We need to build our own specialized agency in order to get a good handle on the flow of that.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2062. Cabinet members have CIRO.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2061. The Self Defense Force has the Ground Staff Office.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2064. And we, the police... have the Public Relations Office.``
I felt a tremble of elation run through my whole body, then rubbed my arms.
...The disappointment and discouragement that had been lingering in my heart until a moment ago were nowhere to be found.
All that remained was a sense of purpose, for the new mission I would been entrusted with...!
changeReason: New translation introduces a grammatical error: 'I would been entrusted' should be 'I would be entrusted' or 'I had been entrusted'.

TextENGNew: ``I'll do it, Yamaoki-san.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2169. I'll do all I can to live up to your expectations.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2170. Also...!``
If we could get the power of the press in our hands, we would even have the power to oppose heads of government.
...It might even be enough to get the ball rolling to avenge Minai-san.
When I thought about that... all the troubles and hardships I would be facing from now on seemed completely painless.
``Then... it's settled.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2066. I don't know how many decades it may take, but I look forward to that day.``

``Hahah, it won't take decades.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2172. Yeah... I'm sure it'll take ten years at most.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2173. I'm sure there will be greatly of difficult challenges in that time... so I'll be counting on you.``
changeReason: New translation introduces a grammatical error: 'there will be greatly of difficult challenges' should be 'a lot of' or 'a great many'.

TextENGNew: As I uttered those words.
I picked up the can of coffee in front of me, pulled the tab, and held it up to Yamaoki-san with a fearless feeling in my heart.
He smiled softly back at me, then picked up his nearly empty can and clanked it into mine.
The hot coffee had already completely cooled off.
But my entire body was filled with burning heat...
